title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of steaming Mercimek Corbasi, an authentic Turkish red lentil soup, garnished with fresh herbs.
seo.imageCaption: Warm up with a bowl of homemade Mercimek Corbasi, the beloved Turkish red lentil soup, perfect for any season.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish red lentil soup, cherished for its comforting warmth and vibrant flavor. This creamy, hearty soup is a staple in Turkish homes, often enjoyed as a starter or a light meal. Its simple yet profound taste makes it a truly satisfying experience.

instructions.title: Prepare Lentils and Aromatics
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils under cold running water until the water runs clear. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Sauté Garlic and Tomato Paste
instructions.text: Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, to deepen its flavor.

instructions.title: Combine Lentils and Liquids
instructions.text: Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot. Pour in the hot water or vegetable broth. Stir well to combine all ingredients.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Bring the soup to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have broken down.

instructions.title: Blend and Season
instructions.text: Remove the pot from heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup directly in the pot until smooth and creamy. Alternatively, carefully transfer portions of the soup to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot. Stir in 1 teaspoon of dried mint, 1 teaspoon of red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed.

instructions.title: Prepare the Butter Topping
instructions.text: In a small pan, melt the butter over medium-low heat. Once melted, add 1 tablespoon of dried mint and 1 teaspoon of red pepper flakes. Cook for about 30 seconds, stirring until fragrant, being careful not to burn the spices.

instructions.title: Serve Mercimek Corbasi
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into individual bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the hot spiced butter over each serving. Garnish with a lemon wedge and fresh parsley if desired. Serve immediately with crusty bread.

tips: For an extra smooth soup, you can pass the blended soup through a fine-mesh sieve after blending.
Always rinse your lentils thoroughly to remove any impurities and help prevent digestive discomfort.
Serve with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice; it brightens the flavor and is a traditional accompaniment.
article:
Mercimek Corbasi: The Heartwarming Red Lentil Soup of Turkey

Step into any Turkish home or restaurant, and chances are you’ll encounter the comforting aroma and vibrant hue of Mercimek Corbasi. This beloved red lentil soup is more than just a dish; it's a staple, a symbol of hospitality, and a testament to the simplicity and richness of Turkish cuisine. From bustling city eateries to quiet village kitchens, Mercimek Corbasi holds a special place, often served as a warming start to a meal or a nourishing dish on its own. Its creamy texture, savory depth, and hint of refreshing mint make it an irresistible choice, adored by locals and visitors alike. If you’re exploring Turkish soups and stews, this classic is undoubtedly where you should begin your culinary journey.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup?

Mercimek Corbasi is a creamy, flavorful red lentil soup that is ubiquitous throughout Turkey. Its name, "mercimek çorbası," literally translates to "lentil soup." What sets it apart is its signature vibrant reddish-orange color, smooth consistency, and the harmonious blend of humble ingredients that create an extraordinarily rich taste. Typically made from red lentils, onions, garlic, tomato paste, and a unique topping of butter infused with red pepper flakes (pul biber) and dried mint, this soup is a masterclass in balancing simplicity with profound flavor.

Often pureed to achieve its characteristic velvety texture, Mercimek Corbasi is both hearty and light. It offers a wonderfully earthy taste from the lentils, brightened by the tangy tomato paste and the aromatic depth of onions and garlic. The final flourish of the mint and pepper-infused butter isn't just for garnish; it adds a crucial layer of flavor, introducing a warm, slightly spicy kick and a refreshing herbal note that elevates the entire dish. It's a comforting bowl that nourishes the body and soothes the soul, making it a perfect choice for any time of day, from a quick lunch to an elegant dinner starter.

## Ingredients You Need for Mercimek Corbasi

The beauty of Mercimek Corbasi lies in its accessible ingredients, many of which you likely already have in your pantry. Here’s what you’ll need to create this Turkish delight:

*   **Red Lentils:** The star of the show. Make sure they are split red lentils, as they cook faster and break down more easily, contributing to the soup's creamy texture.
*   **Onion & Garlic:** The aromatic foundation. A finely chopped yellow onion and a few cloves of minced garlic will provide a sweet and pungent base.
*   **Tomato Paste:** Adds depth of flavor, a beautiful reddish hue, and a touch of acidity.
*   **Flour (Optional but Recommended):** A tablespoon or two of all-purpose flour can be used to create a roux with the oil, helping to thicken the soup and give it a smoother mouthfeel.
*   **Olive Oil or Butter:** For sautéing the aromatics and making the flavorful topping.
*   **Chicken or Vegetable Broth (or Water):** The liquid base. Broth adds more flavor, but water works perfectly well, especially with the other robust ingredients.
*   **Dried Mint (Nane):** Crucial for the distinctive Turkish flavor. It’s used both in the soup and, more prominently, in the butter topping.
*   **Red Pepper Flakes (Pul Biber):** A mild to medium-hot chili flake, providing a gentle warmth and vibrant color to the butter topping.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To season the soup to perfection.
*   **Lemon Wedges:** Essential for serving, providing a burst of fresh acidity that brightens every spoonful.

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Mercimek Corbasi

Making Mercimek Corbasi is a straightforward process, even for novice cooks. Follow these steps to create a delicious and authentic Turkish red lentil soup:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch, which can cause the soup to become cloudy. Drain well.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil or butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent, being careful not to brown them. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
3.  **Build the Base:** Stir in 1-2 tablespoons of tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to deepen its flavor. If using, sprinkle in 1 tablespoon of flour and cook for another minute, stirring to combine it with the oil and aromatics.
4.  **Add Lentils and Liquid:** Add the rinsed and drained red lentils to the pot. Pour in 6-7 cups of chicken or vegetable broth (or water). Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
5.  **Season and Blend:** Once the lentils are cooked, remove the pot from the heat. Stir in 1 teaspoon of dried mint, salt, and black pepper to taste. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup directly in the pot until it is smooth and creamy. If you don't have an immersion blender, transfer the soup in batches to a standard blender, being cautious with hot liquids, and blend until smooth. Return the blended soup to the pot.
6.  **Adjust Consistency:** If the soup is too thick, add a little more broth or hot water until it reaches your desired consistency. Simmer for another 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
7.  **Prepare the Topping (Nane-Pul Biber Sos):** While the soup simmers, melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a small pan over medium-low heat. Once the butter is melted and foamy, remove it from the heat and stir in 1-2 teaspoons of dried mint and 1 teaspoon of red pepper flakes (or more, to taste). The residual heat will toast the spices without burning them.
8.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle a generous spoonful of the fragrant mint and red pepper butter over each serving. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side.

## Pro Tips for the Best Mercimek Corbasi

Achieving the perfect bowl of Mercimek Corbasi is simple with a few expert tricks:

*   **Rinse Those Lentils!** Don't skip rinsing your red lentils. This step is crucial for removing excess starch, ensuring your soup is clear and not gummy.
*   **Don't Rush the Sauté:** Allow the onions to soften properly and the tomato paste to cook out for a few minutes. This deepens the foundational flavors of the soup significantly.
*   **Use an Immersion Blender:** While a regular blender works, an immersion blender makes the process incredibly easy and reduces cleanup. It also allows you to control the smoothness of the soup directly in the pot.
*   **Adjust Consistency to Your Liking:** Some prefer a thicker, almost stew-like soup, while others prefer it thinner. Add more broth or water gradually after blending until you reach your desired consistency.
*   **The Power of the Topping:** The melted butter infused with dried mint and red pepper flakes is non-negotiable for an authentic taste. It adds a layer of richness, warmth, and herbaceousness that truly defines Mercimek Corbasi. Don't add it to the whole pot; drizzle it individually over each bowl for the best effect.
*   **Lemon is Key:** Always serve with lemon wedges. A squeeze of fresh lemon juice just before eating brightens the soup, cuts through the richness, and enhances all the other flavors.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Red Lentil Soup

Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly versatile and pairs well with a variety of accompaniments. Here are some traditional and delicious ways to enjoy your homemade red lentil soup:

*   **Fresh Lemon Wedges:** This is paramount. The acidity of lemon cuts through the richness and brightens the earthy flavors of the lentils.
*   **Crusty Bread:** A staple with any Turkish soup. Serve with slices of fresh, crusty bread or traditional Turkish pide to dip into the rich broth.
*   **A Sprinkle of Herbs:** A garnish of fresh parsley or additional dried mint can add a fresh touch and visual appeal.
*   **Pickles (Tursu):** In Turkey, it's common to serve soups with a side of mixed pickles, which offer a tangy contrast.
*   **As a Starter:** Mercimek Corbasi makes an excellent appetizer for a larger Turkish meal, preparing the palate for main courses like kebabs or stews.
*   **Light Main Course:** Accompanied by bread and a fresh salad, it can easily serve as a satisfying and healthy light lunch or dinner.
*   **Yogurt (Optional):** While not traditional for Mercimek Corbasi itself, a dollop of plain yogurt on the side can be a refreshing addition if you enjoy its creamy tang.

## Variations and Substitutions for Mercimek Corbasi

While the classic Mercimek Corbasi is perfect as is, you can certainly experiment with variations to suit your taste or dietary needs:

*   **Add Vegetables:** For extra nutrition and texture, consider adding finely diced carrots or potatoes along with the lentils. They will cook down and blend seamlessly into the soup.
*   **Spice It Up:** If you like more heat, increase the amount of red pepper flakes in the butter topping, or add a pinch of cayenne pepper to the soup itself. A touch of cumin can also add a warm, earthy note.
*   **Make it Vegan:** This soup is inherently vegan if you use vegetable broth and olive oil instead of butter. Ensure your tomato paste doesn't contain any animal products (which is rare, but always good to check).
*   **Smoky Flavor:** A pinch of smoked paprika can add an interesting depth, though it deviates slightly from the traditional profile.
*   **Different Lentils:** While red lentils are traditional and preferred for their quick cooking and ability to break down, you could experiment with a mix of red and yellow lentils. However, green or brown lentils will result in a different texture and color, as they hold their shape more.
*   **Herb Variations:** While dried mint is the classic, a little fresh dill or parsley stirred in at the end can offer a different aromatic dimension.

Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a soup; it's a culinary hug in a bowl. Its simplicity, combined with its profound flavor and nutritional benefits, makes it a timeless classic in Turkish cuisine. Whether you're seeking comfort, a healthy meal, or an authentic taste of Turkey, this red lentil soup is a dish that promises to delight. Give this recipe a try, and you'll quickly understand why Mercimek Corbasi holds such a cherished place in the hearts and homes across Turkey.
